steps.title: Spot the vertex form — read $(h, k)$ directly
steps.content: $y = -18(x - 7.2)^2 + 680$ is vertex form $a(x-h)^2 + k$. Read off: vertex $= (7.2,\ 680)$. No algebra needed.

steps.title: Since $a = -18 \lt 0$, the vertex is the maximum
steps.content: The parabola opens downward, so the vertex gives the maximum height: 680 ft at $t = 7.2$ s. That is the correct answer.
